heading: Inventory of abandoned vessels
label: 4712 Inventory of abandoned vessels
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, referred to in subsec. (a), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 119–60Dec. 18, 2025

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, the Commandant, in consultation with the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration and relevant State agencies, shall establish and maintain a national inventory of covered vessels that are abandoned.

children.heading: .—Contents
children.label: b .—Contents
children.text: The inventory established and maintained under subsection (a) shall include data on each vessel, including geographic information system data related to the location of each such vessel.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: c .—Publication
children.text: The Commandant shall make the inventory established under subsection (a) publicly available on a website of the Coast Guard.

a State, Indian Tribe, Native Hawaiian organization, or person may report a covered vessel that may be abandoned to the Commandant for potential inclusion in the inventory established under subsection (a);
the Commandant shall review any such report and add such vessel to the inventory if the Commandant determines that the reported vessel is abandoned pursuant to section 4711.

children.heading: .—Clarification
children.label: e .—Clarification
children.text: Except in a response action carried out under section 311(j) of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act () or in the case of imminent threat to life and safety, the Commandant shall not be responsible for removing any covered vessels listed on the inventory established and maintained under subsection (a).33 U.S.C. 1321
